overrides.expect: \begin{array}{r c l} v _ {n} (A, B, y) & = & \sigma_ {n} (A, 0, B, y) \wedge \chi_ {n} (A) \\ & = & \text {"A \subseteq \{0,1,\ldots , n - 1\} and the n - blocks of} \\ & & A \text {   starting at   0   and   } B \text {   starting at   } y \text {   are   the } \\ & & \text { same"} \end{array}
overrides.new: \begin{array}{rcl}v_n(A,B,y)&=&\sigma_n(A,0,B,y)\wedge\chi_n(A)\\&=&\text{"}A\subseteq\{0,1,\ldots,n-1\}\text{ and the n-blocks of}\\&&A\text{ starting at 0 and }B\text{ starting at }y\text{ are the}\\&&\text{ same"}\end{array}

overrides.expect: \begin{array}{r c l} \varphi_ {n 2 ^ {n}} (x, y) & = & \varphi_ {n} (x, y) \wedge \exists B   \psi_ {n} (B) \wedge \tau_ {n} (x, y, B) \\ & = & \text { ``the n - blocks of x and y in B_{n} are the same,} \\ & & \text { and x and y are in the same position in the } \\ & & \text { block; that is, x\equiv y (mod n)" } \\ & = & x \equiv y (\text { mod n2^{n }}). \end{array}
overrides.new: \begin{array}{rcl}\varphi_{n2^n}(x,y)&=&\varphi_n(x,y)\wedge\exists B\ \psi_n(B)\wedge\tau_n(x,y,B)\\&=&\text{``the n-blocks of x and y in }B_n\text{ are the same,}\\&&\text{ and x and y are in the same position in the}\\&&\text{ block; that is, }x\equiv y\ (\text{mod }n)\text{"}\\&=&x\equiv y\ (\text{mod }n2^n).\end{array}

overrides.expect: \begin{array}{l l} N \in \mathrm{m} (\sigma (\rho (\varphi))) \\ \Rightarrow N \in \mathrm{m} (\rho (\sigma (\varphi))) & \text { because } \sigma \text { and } \rho \text { commute } \\ \Rightarrow N \in \mathrm{m} (\rho_ {1} (\rho_ {0} (\sigma (\varphi)))) \\ \Rightarrow N \in \mathrm{m} (\rho_ {1} (\rho_ {0} (\sigma (\varphi))) ^ {- C}) & \text { Lemma   H.2(ii),   var(N,C) = \varnothing} \\ \Rightarrow N \in \mathrm{m} (\rho_ {1} (\rho_ {0} (\sigma (\varphi)) ^ {- C})) & \text { Lemma   H.2(iii), } \rho_ {1} (C) = C. \end{array}

overrides.expect: \begin{array}{l} z \in I _ {n + 1} \wedge x = y z \\ \Leftrightarrow \exists z _ {1} \exists z _ {2} \exists z _ {3} \exists z _ {4} \\ \bigwedge_ {i = 1} ^ {4} z _ {i} \in I _ {n} \wedge z = z _ {1} z _ {2} + z _ {3} + z _ {4} \wedge x = y z _ {1} z _ {2} + y z _ {3} + y z _ {4} \\ \Leftrightarrow \exists z _ {1} \exists z _ {2} \exists z _ {3} \exists z _ {4} \exists s \exists t \exists u \exists v \exists w z = s + z _ {3} + z _ {4} \wedge x = u + v + w \\ \wedge \bigwedge_ {i = 1} ^ {4} z _ {i} \in I _ {n} \wedge s = z _ {1} z _ {2} \wedge t = y z _ {1} \wedge u = t z _ {2} \\ \qquad \qquad \qquad \qquad \qquad \qquad \qquad \qquad \qquad \qquad \qquad \qquad \qquad \qquad \qquad \qquad \qquad \qquad \qquad \qquad \qquad \qquad \qquad \qquad \qquad \qquad \qquad \qquad \qquad \qquad \qquad \qquad \qquad \qquad \\ \qquad \qquad \qquad \qquad \qquad \qquad \qquad \qquad \qquad \qquad \qquad \qquad \qquad \qquad \qquad \qquad \qquad \qquad \qquad \qquad \qquad \qquad \qquad \qquad \qquad \qquad \qquad \qquad \qquad \qquad \qquad \qquad \qend{array}
